data.title: Sterling Remains Vulnerable After U.K. GDP Data — Market Talk
data.description: Sterling remains vulnerable even after U.K. economic data for the fourth quarter and December were better than expected, Monex Europe analysts say in a note. Increased U.K. government spending seems to have supported economic activity but this is an unsustainable method of sustaining growth in the longer term, they say. "It also leaves the Treasury with a headache." Treasury chief Rachel Reeves faces hard decisions for the March budget with her fiscal headroom likely having evaporated based on these latest figures. Sterling is at risk of falling in the coming months even if the U.K. outlook looks relatively more favorable compared to the EU. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

data.title: CATL's Secondary Listing Should Unlock Access to Global Investors, Capital — Market Talk
data.description: Contemporary Amperex Technology's secondary listing in Hong Kong should facilitate access to international capital, global investors and funding for its global expansion, Bernstein analysts write in a note. With 260 billion yuan in cash on the balance sheet, the company doesn't necessarily need the cash, and the listing proceeds will be mainly used to fund the battery maker's 100 GWh Hungary battery plant, they say. CATL is more positive on battery demand growth than Bernstein, with the company's forecast for 2030 25% higher than the asset manager's projection. Bernstein maintains its outperform rating on the stock with a target price of CNY340. Shares last closed at CNY261.39. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

data.title: World Bank to lend Albania $120 mln for resilience, green growth
data.description:
TIRANA (Albania), February 13 (SeeNews) - The World Bank Group said it has approved a $120 million (115.2 million euro) development policy loan (DPL) to Albania to help the country strengthen its fiscal resilience and promote green development.

The programme is expected to reinforce government and household resilience, enhance the energy market framework while scaling up renewable energy and energy efficiency with renewables, and protect the environment and reduce Albania's carbon footprint, the World Bank said in a press release on Wednesday.

“This financing will help the country address critical challenges in the energy sector and environmental protection, while also strengthening the government’s ability to manage fiscal risks and better support vulnerable populations,” Richard Record, World Bank acting country manager for Albania, said in the statement.

This programme aligns with the bank's Country Partnership Framework, supporting Albania’s sustainable development, as well as with the country's EU aspirations, Economic Reform Programme, and Paris Agreement commitments, the banking group said.

($ = 0.960 euro)

data.title: Singapore Shares Close Higher; Grand Banks Yachts Slides 14%
data.description:
Singapore shares closed in green on Thursday as investors shrugged off higher-than-expected US inflation data.

The Straits Times Index ranged between 3,864.38 and 3,882.58 throughout the day. It ended the session at 3,882.58, up 7.96 points or 0.21% compared to Wednesday's close.

In corporate news, shares of Grand Bank Yacht slid nearly 14% even as its attributable profit rose 9.2% to SG$7.6 million in the fiscal first half from SG$6.9 million a year earlier.

Seatrium surged nearly 11% at the close after it signed an MoU with BP Exploration & Production to prepare for a second project at the US Gulf of America.

Meanwhile, shares of Digital Core REIT were up nearly 4% after its distribution per unit (DPU) for the second half of 2024 rose 1.1% to $0.0180 from $0.0178 a year prior.

data.title: Swiss Franc Strengthens
data.description:
The Swiss Franc firmed to around 0.90 per USD, its highest in a week, amid a weakening dollar following news that the US aims to begin talks with Russia to end the war in Ukraine.

Meanwhile, Switzerland's consumer price inflation eased for the second straight month in January to the lowest level in nearly four years at 0.6%.

data.title: Hang Seng Bank Among First to Implement Offshore Renminbi Repurchases
data.description:
Hang Seng Bank is among the first banks to implement offshore renminbi repo on the day of its launch on Feb. 10, according to a same-day news release by the bank.

The transactions utilize eligible onshore bonds held under the Northbound Bond Connect scheme as collateral to help boost offshore renminbi liquidity, according to the bank.

The offshore repurchase agreement is one of the new policy measures by the Hong Kong Monetary Authority and the People's Bank of China to boost financial links with Hong Kong and mainland China, Hang Seng Bank said.

data.title: Fitch Rates MapletreeLog Treasury's Bonds BBB+
data.description:
Fitch Ratings has placed a BBB+ long-term rating on MapletreeLog Treasury's proposed SG$50 million seven-year unsecured notes, according to a Wednesday release.

The issuer is a subsidiary of Singapore-listed Mapletree Logistics Trust , with the notes' rating aligning with the parent's long-term issuer default rating.

HSBC Institutional Trust Services (Singapore) will guarantee the notes as trustee of Mapletree Logistics Trust.

The parent's rating balances its wide scale and high-quality, geographically diversified portfolio of logistics assets with increased EBITDA net leverage, Fitch said.

The trust's varied portfolio should anchor positive portfolio rent reversions and stable cash flow even with a weak China segment, the rating agency said.

Acquisitions should slow down in the next three to four years even with the trust's continued push for a portfolio refresh, Fitch said.

Significant changes in the parent's EBITDA net leverage, net debt to investment property value ratio, and operating conditions including weaker logistics sector fundamentals could trigger future rating actions, according to the rating agency.

data.title: Kepler cuts Cargotec to 'reduce' on MacGregor sale shortfall, 2025 outlook
data.description:
** Kepler Cheuvreux cuts Cargotec's stock to "reduce" from "hold", citing a MacGregor sale shortfall for the Finnish engineering group and its target of flat earnings growth in 2025

** In November, the firm said it sold MacGregor business to funds managed by Triton for 480 million euros ($499.92 million)

** The sale shortfall stems from the buyer classifying Net Working Capital (NWC) as debt, a factor Cargotec hadn't reflected in its initial sale price, Kepler says

** "Net, it leaves Cargotec with 376 million euros or 8.8 euros per share less cash versus our base-case valuation," the broker adds

** Kepler also said it expects a potential rebound for the company's earnings only in 2026-2027

** Shares of Cargotec are down 4.5% and among worst performers on pan-European STOXX 600 Index

($1 = 0.9602 euros)

data.title: Saudi Exchange Sets Fluctuation Limits for Dar Almarkabah For Renting Cars
data.description:
The Saudi Exchange set the fluctuation limits for Dar Almarkabah For Renting Cars (SASE:9577) after shareholders approval for its forward shares split, according to a Thursday release.

The limits will be based on the vehicle rental company's share price of 4 Saudi riyals, with outstanding orders canceled. Saudi Arabia's Edaa will add the shares to the portfolios of investors by Feb. 17.

data.title: Eversource Energy reports results for the quarter ended December 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Eversource Energy reported quarterly adjusted earnings of $1.01​​ per share for the quarter ended December 31, lower than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of $1.36. The mean expectation of sixteen analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 99 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 96 cents to $1.01 per share.

  • Revenue rose 10.3% to $2.97 billion from a year ago; analysts expected $3.14 billion.

  • Eversource Energy's reported EPS for the quarter was 20 cents​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $72.52 million.

  • Eversource Energy shares had risen by 4.0% this quarter.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 0.5% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, three analysts negatively revised earnings estimates

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 10 "strong buy" or "buy," 8 "hold" and 2 "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The average consensus recommendation for the electric utilities peer group is also "buy"

Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Eversource Energy is $71.00

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data February 13 at 09:39 a.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: Cisco shares rise on strong demand for AI gear as Trump tariffs weigh
data.description:
Cisco Systems shares rose 6.4% in premarket trading on Thursday after the company raised its annual revenue forecast on robust demand for its cloud networking gear, while addressing any potential impact from the latest U.S. tariffs.

Demand for the telecom equipment maker's ethernet switches and routers used in data centers has surged as companies ramp up their investments in artificial-intelligence infrastructure.

Despite macro uncertainty, Cisco is seeing strong cloud demand including triple-digit order growth from hyperscalers and improving demand from telco customers to equip their networks for AI-led traffic demand, said J.P.Morgan analysts led by Samik Chatterjee.

U.S. businesses have warned of the fallout from the tariffs President Donald Trump has imposed on Canada, Mexico and China, as well as on steel and aluminum. Levies on Canada and Mexico have been paused for a month, but not on China.

Chief Financial Officer Scott Herren said on a conference call that the company's adjusted gross margin forecast for the third quarter has the cost of the proposed tariffs built into it.

"We would expect the majority of the reason for the slightly weaker gross margin outlook for Q3 is due to expected tariff impact in the near term," said Morgan Stanley analysts.

At least six analysts have raised their price targets on the stock, according to data compiled by LSEG.

Cisco has a 12-month forward price-to-earnings ratio of 16.23, compared with Arista Networks 43.21. Cisco has gained over 17% in 2024, while ANET has risen more than 87% in the same period.

data.title: Tele2, Delivery Hero's foodora Launch Food, Grocery Delivery by Drone in Sweden
data.description:
Tele2 (TEL2-B.ST, TEL2-A.ST) and Delivery Hero's foodora will begin food and grocery deliveries by drone in Värmdö and Gustavsberg in Sweden.

The first delivery is scheduled for Friday, with the primary delivery area covering up to 10 kilometers in diameter, with potential for expansion up to 15 kilometers, according to a Thursday release. A phased expansion of delivery areas is planned.

The Swedish telecom group supplied 5G connectivity for the drones developed by Norwegian drone technology company Aviant.

data.title: Czech Republic Current Account Shifts to Surplus
data.description:
The Czech Republic’s current account posted a surplus of CZK 15.19 billion in December 2024, shifting from a CZK 7.29 billion deficit in the same month of the previous year and exceeding expectations of an CZK 8.9 billion excess.

The surplus in both the goods and services accounts widened to CZK 21.24 billion and CZK 9.42 billion, respectively, from CZK 18.14 billion and CZK 5.69 billion in December 2023.

Meanwhile, the primary income deficit narrowed significantly to CZK 9.74 billion from CZK 32.43 billion.

In contrast, the secondary income account recorded a deficit of CZK 5.72 billion, reversing from a CZK 1.30 billion surplus in the corresponding period last year.

data.title: Konecranes Bags Order for Electric Mobile Harbor Cranes in Belgium
data.description:
Konecranes received an order for four all-electric mobile harbor cranes from Belgian logistics company Katoen Natie, according to a Thursday release.

The Finnish crane manufacturer will deliver two ESP.9 Konecranes Gottwald and two ESP.8 Konecranes Gottwald to the client's operations in the Port of Antwerp, Belgium, in the first quarter of 2026. The order also includes a 10-year service level agreement.

data.title: SailPoint Prices Upsized Nasdaq IPO of 60 Million Shares
data.description:
SailPoint (SAIL) said early Thursday it priced upsized its initial public offering of 60 million shares at $23 apiece.

The shares will likely begin trading on the Nasdaq Global Select Market on Feb. 13 under the "SAIL" ticker. The offering will probably close on Feb. 14.

The cybersecurity firm plans to issue and sell 57.5 million shares, and certain existing stockholders will offload the remaining 2.5 million. On Feb. 4, SailPoint launched its roadshow for an IPO of 50 million shares, and it expected a pricing of between $19 and $21 per share.

SailPoint has granted underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to 9 million additional shares.

The net proceeds will be used to repay a portion of its term loan, settle outstanding equity awards and appreciation rights, and pay the outstanding fees under the advisory services agreement with Thoma Bravo.

data.title: Abu Dhabi Aviation to Halt Trading on Abu Dhabi Bourse
data.description:
Abu Dhabi Aviation (ADX:ADAVIATION) will temporarily cease trading on the Abu Dhabi Securities Exchange at 1 pm local time on Feb. 20 due to a board meeting.

The aviation services company's shares will resume trading after it discloses the meeting results, according to a Thursday release.

data.title: Palestine Inflation Rate Eases to 1-Year Low
data.description:
The annual inflation rate in Palestine slowed for the second consecutive month to 22.77% in January 2025 from 87.99% in December 2024.

This marked the lowest inflation rate since January 2024, mainly influenced by the announcement of a ceasefire in the Gaza Strip.

On a monthly basis, consumer prices decreased 53.27% in January 2025, reversing from a 2.97% increase in December.

data.title: Jefferies Adjusts Bharat Forge's Price Target to INR950 From INR1,100, Keeps at Underperform
data.description: Bharat Forge has an average rating of hold and price targets ranging from 920 Indian rupees to 1,839 rupees, according to analysts polled by Capital IQ.

data.title: Chuanzhiboke Education Technology Completes Acquisition of 51% Stake in YouYou HuiLian
data.description:
Jiangsu Chuanzhiboke Education Technology completed its acquisition of a 51% stake in YouYou HuiLian for 106.4 million yuan.

The move supports the online training course provider's goal of expanding its vocational education offerings, particularly in e-commerce and cross-border trade disciplines, for higher education institutions, according to a Thursday filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

Shares of Jiangsu Chuanzhiboke Education Technology closed less than 1% higher Thursday.

data.title: Nissan Projects Annual Net Loss as It Faces Weak Sales, Takes Restructuring Steps
data.description:
(08:42 GMT) *Nissan Motor 3Q Loss Y14.1B Vs. Net Y29.1B >7201.TO
(08:42 GMT) *Nissan Motor 3Q Rev Y3.159T Vs. Y3.108T >7201.TO
(08:43 GMT) *Nissan 3Q Net Loss Missed Estimated Net Profit of Y5.68B in Poll of Analysts by LSEG
(08:45 GMT) *Nissan Cuts Revenue, Operating-Profit Views for FY Ending March
(08:51 GMT) *Nissan: To Actively Explore New Partnerships
(08:52 GMT) *Nissan: To Cut Top Management Positions by 20%
(08:54 GMT) *Nissan Maintains FY Global Sales Forecast at 3.4M Units
(09:30 GMT) Nissan Projects Annual Net Loss as It Faces Weak Sales, Takes Restructuring Steps

By Kosaku Narioka

Nissan Motor projected a full-year net loss as the automaker took restructuring steps to address weak sales.

The Japanese automaker said Thursday that it booked a net loss of 14.1 billion yen, equivalent to $91.3 million, for the three months ended Dec. 31, compared with a net profit of Y29.1 billion in the year-earlier period. That missed the estimated net profit of Y5.68 billion in a poll of analysts by data provider LSEG.

Third-quarter revenue increased 1.6% to Y3.159 trillion, even as global vehicle sales dropped.

Nissan is in the midst of turnaround efforts partly to address weak sales, especially in China. The company said in November that it planned to cut 9,000 jobs globally and reduce global production capacity by a fifth.

Nissan and Honda on Thursday officially scrapped their planned merger less than two months after announcing it, putting pressure on troubled Nissan to look for other partnerships.

Nissan now expects a Y80.0 billion net loss for the year ending March. It retracted its previous annual net profit projection in November, saying it was examining the costs required for restructuring efforts.

The company continues to project global sales to drop 1.2% to 3.4 million vehicles this fiscal year.

Write to Kosaku Narioka at kosaku.narioka@wsj.com
